trm:term.trm:name: XPath Query
trm:term.trm:definition: A mechanism for running XPath 1.0 expressions against the combined spec document to extract terms, definitions, relations, artifact mappings, and LLM descriptions programmatically. The query command pre-registers all namespace prefixes (spec, pr, trm, org, rel, art, llm, rev, idx, cmb) so that XPath expressions can reference any layer without manual namespace declaration. Results are returned as raw XML fragments, plain text (with --text), or counts (with --count), making them suitable for agent pipelines and shell scripting.

pr:section.pr:title: XPath Query Tooling
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How the CLI query command turns the combined spec document into a queryable knowledge base.
pr:section.pr:p: The query command assembles all layer files into a single combined document (including XSLT-synthesized relations), converts it to lxml for full XPath 1.0 support, and evaluates the given expression with all namespace prefixes pre-registered. This lets agents trace from a concept to its definition, its code, its dependencies, and its machine description in a single pipeline.
The command accepts an XPath expression and an optional spec directory path:

pr:property.pr:propdesc: Extract text content only, stripping XML tags. Useful for definitions, titles, and LLM descriptions.
Print only the count of matching nodes. Useful for CI checks and agent scripts.

pr:section.pr:p: Return types are handled automatically: element results are serialized as XML fragments (or text with --text), while scalar results (attribute values, strings, numbers, booleans) are printed as plain text. Invalid XPath expressions produce a clean error message and exit code 1, allowing agents to distinguish bad queries from empty results.

Because the query runs against the post-XSLT combined document, it sees all synthesized reference relations in addition to explicitly authored relations. This means queries like //rel:relation[@from="some-section"] will find both hand-written and auto-generated edges.
pr:section.pr:note: XPath 1.0 only: no regex, no if/then/else. Use contains(), starts-with(), and substring() for string matching within XPath expressions.

pr:example.pr:title: Find code paths mapped to a spec node

diag:issue.diag:title: XPath returns no results
diag:issue.diag:condition: A query succeeds but prints no matching nodes.
diag:issue.diag:cause: The expression may use the wrong namespace prefix, query a raw file shape instead of the combined document shape, or target an ID that is not present in the selected spec.
diag:issue.diag:remedy: Start with a count query such as //pr:section, confirm the relevant namespace prefix, then narrow the predicate.

org:task.org:purpose: The CLI query command evaluates XPath 1.0 expressions against the combined spec document, enabling programmatic navigation of the knowledge base.

rel:relation.rel:note: The query command depends on combined document assembly to produce the queryable XML tree.
The diagnostic issue describes a query failure mode.
The issue is represented in the diagnostic layer.
The query tooling section depends on the XPath query concept for its semantic foundation.

art:mapping.art:note: query() click command: assembles combined doc, converts to lxml, evaluates XPath, handles --text and --count flags.
The query command is the canonical implementation of the XPath query concept.

spec:clayers.llm:node: The query command assembles all spec layer files into a combined document (with XSLT reference synthesis), converts to lxml, and evaluates an XPath 1.0 expression with all namespace prefixes pre-registered. Elements are output as XML fragments; scalars as plain text. --text strips XML tags for plain text extraction. --count prints the number of matching nodes. Invalid XPath gives exit code 1. The command enables agents to trace concepts to definitions, code, dependencies, and LLM descriptions in a single pipeline.

XPath querying is the mechanism for programmatic access to the combined spec document. All namespace prefixes (spec, pr, trm, org, rel, art, llm, rev, idx, cmb) are pre-registered, so expressions like //trm:term[@id="X"]/trm:definition work without manual namespace setup. The query command converts the stdlib ET combined doc to lxml for full XPath 1.0 support (stdlib ET has very limited XPath). Results can be XML fragments, plain text (--text), or counts (--count).
